title: Appointment of Governor
text:
The Governor is appointed by the President under Article 155.
The Governor holds office during the pleasure of the President under Article 156.
Normally the tenure is five years.

title: Powers of Governor
text:
The Governor exercises executive, legislative, financial and judicial powers.
The Governor also has discretionary powers in specific situations.
retrieval_text:

title: Discretionary Powers
text:
The Governor can reserve Bills for the President under Article 200,
recommend President's Rule under Article 356,
and invite a party to form the government when no clear majority exists.

title: Governor and Centre-State Relations
text:
The Governor acts as the constitutional link between the Union and the State.
The Governor sends reports to the President regarding constitutional machinery in the State.
